---
title: "Setting up Ubuntu for Geocomputation"
date: 2023-04-11
categories: [news]
tags: [ubuntu, R, geocomputation]
description: "Setting up a new computer with Ubuntu for spatial data analysis"
---

I recently got a new laptop and set it up with Ubuntu for spatial data analysis. Here's my approach.

## Why Ubuntu?

- Free and open source
- Excellent for R and Python development
- Great support for spatial packages
- Reliable and fast

## Key steps

1. Install Ubuntu (I use LTS versions)
2. Install R and RStudio
3. Add spatial packages: `sf`, `terra`, `igraph`
4. Set up Python with conda/mamba
5. Install QGIS for visualization

## Resources

See my [install-geocomp-ubuntu](https://github.com/Robinlovelace/install-geocomp-ubuntu) repo for detailed setup scripts.
